codeReview.summary: The fix closes the prior routing gap: cmd/worker now wires provider selection through a ProviderRegistry keyed by EffectivePolicy.ProviderRoute, and regression tests exercise runtime route selection, end-to-end persistence/audit behavior, and fallback under policy-driven routing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo already relies on `cmd/worker/main.go` to seed `rules.PlatformDefaults`, including the default provider route, but this runtime-policy source of truth is only captured in `.factory/library/integrations.md` and not in mission guidance. Workers extending provider routing could miss that runtime defaults and policy override behavior originate there.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/integrations.md:34 notes that `cmd/worker/main.go` seeds `rules.PlatformDefaults`; cmd/worker/main.go wires `platformDefaultRoute` into `rules.PlatformDefaults.ProviderRoute` and the runtime provider registry.

summary: Review passed: the fix removes hard-coded runtime provider routing, proves policy-driven provider selection in processor/runtime tests, and adequately addresses the original failure in beta-provider-routing-rate-limits. The repo is not fully clean because a pre-existing untracked repo-root `worker` binary remains, but that residue was already present before this fix and is called out in the handoff rather than introduced by this feature.
